description: Column specification and methodology documentation for SCVR output Parquet files stored in data/processed/scvr/<site_id>/.
format: Apache Parquet, long format (one row = one variable × one center_year combination)

methodology.definition: SCVR (Severe Climate Variability Rating) quantifies the fractional increase in the area under the empirical exceedance curve for a future climate period compared to the historical baseline. A positive SCVR means extreme events are more frequent or more severe than the baseline.
methodology.exceedance_area: Computed using the trapezoidal rule over rank-sorted daily values: area = trapz(sorted_values, linspace(0, 1, n))
methodology.window_approach: Future values are drawn from a 20-year window centered on each target year (e.g., 2030 uses data from 2020–2040). This suppresses inter-annual natural variability and isolates the climate trend signal.
methodology.baseline_period: 1980–2014 (ERA5-calibrated). All exceedance thresholds and percentiles are derived from this period.
methodology.reference: Methodology aligned with team proposal in docs/drive_docs/LT Risk Evolution Framework.md

interpretation.scvr_zero: No change from baseline — future distribution identical to historical.
interpretation.scvr_positive: Extremes are more frequent or more intense (e.g., more hot days, more heavy rain).
interpretation.scvr_negative: Extremes are less frequent (e.g., fewer freeze days under warming — important for icing risk).

magnitude_guide.above_0.60: Very high change (>60%) — review carefully, may indicate tail sensitivity

center_year.description: Center year of the 20-year future window

variable.description: Climate variable identifier matching variables.json keys

scvr.description: SCVR value: fractional change in exceedance area vs baseline

area_baseline.description: Area under baseline exceedance curve (trapz integral)

area_future.description: Area under future exceedance curve (trapz integral)

window_start_year.description: First year of future data window used (center_year - 10)

window_end_year.description: Last year of future data window used (center_year + 10)

n_baseline_days.description: Number of valid (non-null) baseline days used in computation

n_future_days.description: Number of valid (non-null) future days used in computation

downstream_use.hcr_computation: SCVR feeds into HCR (Hazard Change Ratio) via hazard-specific scaling factors derived from published literature (notebook 02).
downstream_use.efr_computation: SCVR feeds into EFR (Equipment Failure Ratio) via Peck's Model, Coffin-Manson, and Palmgren-Miner engineering models (notebook 02).
downstream_use.nav_impairment: SCVR is the central hub connecting climate projections to financial impact (BI losses and IUL shortening).
